description: A cognitive psychology instructor has collected Attentional Blink (RSVP) data from 25 students, located at ~/pebl/data/ab_data.csv. The file contains columns: participant, trial, lag (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 8), t1_correct (1 or 0), and t2_correct (1 or 0). Analyze the data to compute the conditional T2|T1 accuracy (the proportion of trials where T2 was correct among only those trials where T1 was also correct) at each lag and the Attentional Blink (AB) magnitude (max T2|T1 across lags - min T2|T1 across lags) for each participant. Exclude any participant with 100% T2|T1 accuracy at all lags. Output a JSON report to ~/pebl/analysis/ab_report.json containing a 'participants' list where each entry includes 'id', 't1_accuracy', a 't2_given_t1' dictionary keyed by lag '1' through '8', and 'ab_magnitude', or an 'excluded': true flag with a 'reason'. The report must also include a 'group_means' dictionary containing the average 't2_given_t1' for each lag and the average 'ab_magnitude' across all valid participants.
